title: Tesla caps employee AI spending at $200/week except for Grok
summary:
Tesla told staff it will impose a $200-per-week limit on employee AI spending starting July 6, according to an internal memo reported by The Information (paywall).

The cap lands just months after Tesla pushed employees to use AI more aggressively, a sign that even companies betting their future on the technology are struggling to control its costs.

title: Iberdrola kicks off its first large-scale battery project in the US
summary: Spanish renewable energy and electricity giant Iberdrola is constructing its first battery energy storage project in the US as utilities race to add more storage to strengthen the grid.

title: The Hyundai IONIQ 3 is the affordable electric hatch we want but can’t have
summary: The IONIQ 3 is a high-tech, fully electric hatch that the US will miss out on. It can drive over 300 miles on a single charge and is Hyundai’s first vehicle in Europe to use its new Pleos Connect infotainment system.

title: ‘World’s largest’ second-life EV battery factory opens in just six weeks
summary: Moment Energy has officially opened what it says is the world’s largest second-life EV battery factory, bringing new battery energy storage manufacturing capacity online just six weeks after announcing the project.

title: Polestar EVs are a smoking deal at $25k off now that they’re banned in the US
summary: On the back of the Commerce Department’s incredibly stupid move to ban Polestar from selling EVs in the US, the Polestar 4 is now nearly the cheapest EV in America after a $25k discount, and the Polestar 3 has a pretty great discount applied too.

title: Subaru’s 375-hp AWD Trailseeker EV starts under $40,000
summary: The Trailseeker was Subaru’s best-selling electric SUV last month. Starting at under $40,000, the “go-anywhere, rugged SUV” offers a spacious interior, standard AWD, and a combined 375 horsepower.

title: Tesla launches Model Y L in US — 6 seats, 325 miles, $61,990
summary:
Tesla has launched the Model Y L, its stretched, three-row, six-seat SUV, in the US and Puerto Rico. The automaker confirmed the launch on Thursday, with the vehicle now configurable online.

The Model Y L arrives first as a “Launch Series” priced at $61,990 — more than Tesla’s own Model Y Performance and both of its main three-row EV rivals.

title: Velotric + Heybike July 4th e-bike sales with up to $828 savings, Samsung Bespoke AI ventless washer/dryer $1,850, Greenworks, more
summary:
Leading our Thursday Green Deals is Velotric’s 4th of July Sale with up to $812 e-bike savings, which is offering another chance to score the Fold 1 Plus Folding e-bike with SensorSwap tech and Apple Find My at its $1,399 low, among other models. There’s also Heybike’s own July 4th e-bike sale with up to $828 savings that starts from $999 prices, as well as Samsung’s Bespoke AI All-in-One Ventless Washer/Dryer Combo down at $1,850, deals on a 60V Greenworks pole saw and the 600 CFM Worx Trivac, and much more waiting for you below. There are also all the hangover deals we’ve collected at the bottom of the page for you, like yesterday’s new low prices on the Juiced Scrambler e-bike bundles, the Baseus X1 Pro dual-tracking solar security camera post-Prime Day deal, and more.

Head below for other New Green Deals we’ve found today and, of course, Electrek’s best EV buying and leasing deals. Also, check out the new Electrek Tesla Shop for the best deals on Tesla accessories.

title: Kia axed its affordable EV, but a replacement is set to go on sale soon
summary: Kia is dropping the Niro EV from its 2027 US lineup and will only sell the hybrid version. The EV3 will replace it later this year as Kia’s most affordable electric vehicle.

title: Hydrogen for heavy trucks is dead, says world’s 2nd-largest oil company
summary: Hydrogen heavy trucks are being displaced by electric ones, as advancements in batteries and charging infrastructure eat away at the advantages of hydrogen, according to Sinopec’s internal magazine.

title: Polestar EVs Are A Whopping $25,000 Off After The Brand Got Kicked Out Of America
summary: You can get a Polestar 4 for Chevy Equinox EV money as the company looks to clear out inventory.

title: This Is Tesla's New, Bigger Model Y For The U.S.
summary: The Model Y L features an extended wheelbase, more interior space, and 325 miles of range.

title: BYD Reclaimed The EV Sales Crown Despite Tesla's Huge Quarter
summary: Tesla posted one of its strongest quarters yet, but BYD still managed to reclaim the global EV sales crown.

title: Lucid Just Had A Big Executive Shakeup. Here's What You Need To Know
summary: Last month, Lucid said goodbye to its top engineer. Now it's bringing in a new leadership team.

title: This $1.4 Million EV Has A Weird Trick To Hit 60 MPH In 1.55 Seconds
summary: The McMurtry Spéirling Pure gets 1,000 hp, a 100-kWh battery, and fans that suck it down to the track.

title: Slate Has No Plans To Sell Its Cheap EV Pickup In Canada
summary: Slate's affordable electric pickup won't be headed north any time soon.

title: Someone Keeps Stealing Entire Truckloads Of Tesla Batteries
summary: Thieves have made off with trailers loaded with Tesla battery packs straight from the factory nearly a dozen times since December.

title: California's New EV Incentive Is Almost Here—With Special Benefits For Rivian And Lucid
summary: One key restriction on the $135 million incentive program won't apply to vehicles from California-based EV brands.

title: BMW iX5 Blows Away The Competition With 460-kW Charging, 435-Mile Range
summary: The iX5 gets the largest battery pack ever installed in an electric BMW.

title: An App Flagged This Tesla Battery As Degraded. The Car Said It Was Worse
summary: A four-year-old Model 3 Performance scored 88% battery health in Tesla's own test—lower than its owner expected, but close to a prediction by a third-party app.
